title: Jog Falls
tagline: Jog Falls — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Jog Falls (also known as Gerusoppa Falls, Joga Jalapata, or Jogada Gundi) is a series of large, segmented waterfalls on the Sharavati River in Karnataka state of India, created by
overview.description: Jog Falls (also known as Gerusoppa Falls, Joga Jalapata, or Jogada Gundi) is a series of large, segmented waterfalls on the Sharavati River in Karnataka state of India, created by the river plunging approximately 253 metres (830 ft) and splitting into four distinct cascades (Raja, Rani, Roarer, and Rocket). The waterfalls are situated in the Sagar taluk of the Shivamogga district. Jog Falls is a major attraction for tourists. It features two primary viewpoints: the first one is the front panoramic viewpoint located in the Jog-Kargal Town of Sagara taluk in the Shivamogga district and the secon

topPlaces.name: Christ the King Church, Jog Falls
topPlaces.description: Christ the King Church, Jog Falls was established in 1959. It is located in Jog Falls, Sagara, Shimoga District, Karnataka state, India.

image.alt: Christ the King Church, Jog Falls

topPlaces.name: Kargal, Karnataka
topPlaces.description: Kargal also known as Jog-Kargal is a town in the Indian state of Karnataka, It is known for its proximity to Jog Falls which is situated on the outskirts of the town, The town is located on the banks of River Sharavati and gained importance after the construction of Linganamakki Dam in the year 1954. Kargal was first constituted as notified area and later upgraded as Kargal Town Panchayat on 2001.

topPlaces.name: Sharavathi Pumped Storage Hydropower Project
topPlaces.description: The Sharavathi Pumped Storage Hydropower Project (SPSHP) is a proposed pumped storage power plant proposed to be built on the course of the Sharavathi river in the Indian state of Karnataka in the valley of the Western ghats. It would have an installed capacity of 2000MWe and would use Francis turbines.

topPlaces.name: Talavata
topPlaces.description: Talavata is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the border of Shimoga district and Uttara Kannada District.

topPlaces.name: Linganamakki Dam
topPlaces.description: The Linganamakki Dam was constructed by the Karnataka State Government in 1964. Located in the Linganamakki village of Sagara taluk, the dam has a length of 2.74 kilometres (1.70 mi) stretching across the Sharavathi river.

topPlaces.name: Gerusoppa
topPlaces.description: Gerusoppa is a village in Honnavar Taluk in Uttara Kannada District in the Indian state of Karnataka. The village is also known as Nagarbastikeri and comes under Nagarbastikeri Panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Honnemaradu
topPlaces.description: Honnemaradu is a village situated on the back waters of River Sharavathi in India. Honnemardu derived its name from the Honne tree.

items.note: Christ the King Church, Jog Falls was established in 1959. It is located in Jog Falls, Sagara, Shimoga District, Karnataka state, India.

Kargal also known as Jog-Kargal is a town in the Indian state of Karnataka, It is known for its proximity to Jog Falls which is situated on the outskirts of the town, The town is located on the banks of River Sharavati and gained importance after the construction of Linganamakki Dam in the year 1954. Kargal was first constituted as notified area and later upgraded as Kargal Town Panchayat on 2001.

The Sharavathi Pumped Storage Hydropower Project (SPSHP) is a proposed pumped storage power plant proposed to be built on the course of the Sharavathi river in the Indian state of Karnataka in the valley of the Western ghats. It would have an installed capacity of 2000MWe and would use Francis turbines.
Talavata is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the border of Shimoga district and Uttara Kannada District.

The Linganamakki Dam was constructed by the Karnataka State Government in 1964. Located in the Linganamakki village of Sagara taluk, the dam has a length of 2.74 kilometres (1.70 mi) stretching across the Sharavathi river.
Gerusoppa is a village in Honnavar Taluk in Uttara Kannada District in the Indian state of Karnataka. The village is also known as Nagarbastikeri and comes under Nagarbastikeri Panchayat.
Honnemaradu is a village situated on the back waters of River Sharavathi in India. Honnemardu derived its name from the Honne tree.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jog Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jog Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jog Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (129 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (128 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jog Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jog Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jog Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Jog Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jog Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Christ the King Church, Jog Falls, Kargal, Karnataka, Sharavathi Pumped Storage Hydropower Project, Talavata, Linganamakki Dam.
